name: Southern Pecan Pie
description: A true southern classic, you can't beat a pecan pie! This semi-homemade recipe is super simple and so delicious, it's the perfect holiday pie recipe.

author.name: Rosie
author.description: I’m a Soul Food blogger living in the Pacific Northwest, bringing Southern cooking to gorgeous Seattle, Washington. I was born and raised in the PNW, but my family roots go all the way to Baton Rouge, Louisiana, where my grandparents were born and raised. I live every day to bring the traditional values, recipes, and love of food to life for my family—whether they’re online or in real life. So, welcome to the party, family!

recipeIngredient.name: 1 9-inch deep dish pie shell (store-bought or homemade)

recipeInstructions.text: In a large bowl, add the eggs and mix until everything is one color.
Next, add in the sugar, corn syrup, melted butter, vanilla, and nutmeg. Mix until well combined.
Grab the pie shell and sprinkle the chopped pecans into the center.
Next, pour the pecan pie filling over the pecans.
Place the pie on a cookie sheet and place in a preheated oven. Bake the pie for 45-50 minutes.
Remove and let cool completely before serving. Refrigerate the pie after 2 hours.

video.name: Southern Pecan Pie Recipe: How to Make a Southern Pecan Pie | I Heart Recipes
video.description: Hey y’all! Today I’m sharing a step-by-step guide for a classic holiday dessert. Southern Pecan Pie! https://iheartrecipes.com/southern-pecan-pie-easy/ Not to brag, but this is one of the best pecan pie recipes around! I’m not stingy with the ingredients. I use lots of pecans and plenty of eggs (no runny custard around here, y’all!). Bourbon vanilla extract and a bit of nutmeg give this pie a great flavor. I use store-bought crust to save time, and to make things a little bit easier on myself during the busy holidays, but you can definitely make a crust from scratch. Let’s get baking! Beat together eggs, sugar and melted butter. Add in light corn syrup, nutmeg and bourbon vanilla extract and mix until well combined. Add the chopped pecans to the bottom of a pie shell, top with the egg mixture, and bake for 50 minutes. Once the pie is completely cooled, it’s time to dig in, fam! Find the recipe here: https://iheartrecipes.com/southern-pecan-pie-easy/ Timestamps for video: 0:12 - Add eggs to a bowl. 0:18 - Whisk the eggs. 0:24 - Add granulated sugar. 0:31 - Add melted butter. 0:35 - Pour in light corn syrup. 0:38 - Sprinkle in ground nutmeg. 0:44 - Add bourbon vanilla. 0:47 - Mix well. 0:55 - Add pecans to the pie shell. 1:10 - Pour the liquid into the pie shell. 1:20 - Bake until done. 1:40 - Cool, slice and serve. Loved this Southern Pecan Pie Recipe? Here are some more recipes to try: Homemade Pecan Pie Bars: https://iheartrecipes.com/homemade-pecan-pie-bars/ Caramel Pecan Cinnamon Rolls: https://iheartrecipes.com/caramel-pecan-cinnamon-rolls-scratch/ Pecan Praline Candy: https://iheartrecipes.com/pecan-praline-candy-recipe/ Old Fashioned Chess Pie: https://iheartrecipes.com/old-fashioned-chess-pie/ ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ About Me & I Heart Recipes: Hey there, I'm Rosie - Welcome to my YouTube Cooking Channel. I love cooking, baking, and sharing my recipes. I'm a firm believer that cooking shouldn't be a chore, IT SHOULD BE FUN!!! Therefore, I'm here to share my fun, and easy recipes for everything including soul food favorites, old fashioned southern cuisine, restaurant copycat recipes, and your favorite comfort foods.
